title: BudsAuth: Toward Gesture-Wise Continuous User Authentication Through Earbuds Vibration Sensing

method.input_representation: 6-channel time series (accelerometer x,y,z and gyroscope x,y,z)
method.training_strategy: Domain Adversarial Learning to minimize inter-gesture variance and maximize inter-identity variance; transfer learning for user registration fine-tuning.

evaluation.context: Continuous authentication using weighted voting on 7 consecutive samples of interactive gestures

evaluation_protocol: Cross-session evaluation: samples from the first session used for training and samples from the second session used for testing.

reported_limitations: difficulty accessing raw IMU data from commercial earbuds

usability.usability_issues: Requires user to perform specific on-face interaction gestures; limited accessibility to raw sensor data in commercial hardware.

key_contribution: Proposed BudsAuth, the first system using on-face interaction gestures captured by earbud IMUs for continuous implicit authentication.
Introduced a CNN-based feature encoder with Domain Adversarial Learning (DAL) to decouple identity from gesture behavior.
Developed a distance-based weighted voting strategy for high-precision continuous authentication.
main_strength: Extremely low EER (0.0003) when using consecutive samples.
Low energy consumption and storage overhead.
main_weakness: Evaluation performed in a seated position, lacking robustness tests against real-world motion.
Dependency on specific interaction gestures rather than purely passive signals.
insights.notes_for_review: The system effectively demonstrates the potential of ear-worn IMUs for behavioral biometrics but requires validation in uncontrolled environments to address the identified motion interference.
